name: Grade 8 Hex Cap Screw 1/2-13 x 3" Zinc Yellow
description: Grade 8 hex cap screw made from medium carbon alloy steel, quenched and tempered to 150,000 psi minimum tensile strength. This 1/2-13 x 3-inch bolt with zinc yellow finish provides high-strength fastening for heavy equipment and structural applications.
shortDescription: Grade 8 hex bolt, 1/2-13 x 3", zinc yellow, 150 ksi tensile.
technicalDescription: SAE J429 Grade 8 hex cap screw with full thread. Medium carbon alloy steel (4140/4340), heat treated to HRC 33-39. Zinc yellow dichromate plating per ASTM B633. UNC 2A thread per ASME B1.1.
features: SAE J429 Grade 8 rating (150,000 psi min tensile)
Six-lobe head for positive wrench engagement
benefits: Highest standard fastener grade for demanding applications
seoTitle: Grade 8 Hex Cap Screw 1/2-13 x 3" | 150 ksi
seoDescription: SAE J429 Grade 8 hex bolt, 1/2-13 x 3", 4140 alloy steel, zinc yellow, 150,000 psi tensile. For heavy equipment and structural fastening.

faq.question: What torque should be applied?
faq.answer: Recommended tightening torque for Grade 8 1/2-13 bolt is 90-110 ft-lbs with lubricated threads.

faq.question: Can this be used for structural steel?
faq.answer: Yes, Grade 8 bolts meet structural requirements for steel-to-steel connections in industrial applications.

faq.question: What is the temperature rating?
faq.answer: Grade 8 is rated for service temperatures up to +200°C (+400°F). Beyond this, strength decreases.

name: 316 Stainless Steel Heavy Hex Nut 5/8-11 UNC
description: 316 stainless steel heavy hex nut for corrosive environments. Heat code traceable with full material certification. Ideal for marine, chemical, and pharmaceutical applications where corrosion resistance is critical.
shortDescription: 316 SS heavy hex nut, 5/8-11 UNC, corrosion resistant, full traceability.
technicalDescription: ASTM A194 Grade 8M heavy hex nut. 316 stainless steel (UNS S31600) with full material certification per EN 10204 3.1. UNC 2B thread per ASME B1.1. Passivated per ASTM A967.
features: 316 stainless steel for maximum corrosion resistance
Full material traceability with heat code
Heavy hex pattern for high torque applications
benefits: Superior corrosion resistance in salt and chemical environments
seoTitle: 316 Stainless Steel Heavy Hex Nut 5/8-11 UNC
seoDescription: ASTM A194 8M heavy hex nut, 316 SS, 5/8-11 UNC, passivated, full traceability. For marine, chemical, and pharmaceutical applications.

faq.question: What is the difference between 304 and 316?
faq.answer: 316 contains molybdenum, providing superior pitting and crevice corrosion resistance in chloride environments.

faq.question: Can these nuts be used with Grade 8 bolts?
faq.answer: Yes, but the assembly strength is limited by the lower strength stainless nut. For full Grade 8 strength, use Grade 8 nuts.

faq.question: What lubricant is recommended?
faq.answer: Anti-seize compound is recommended for 316 stainless to prevent galling during installation.

name: Grade 8 Hardened Washer 1.5" ID (Box of 100)
description: SAE J429 Grade 8 hardened flat washer for use with Grade 8 bolts in high-strength applications. Heat treated to HRC 38-45 to prevent embedment under high clamp loads.
shortDescription: Grade 8 hardened washer, 1.5" ID, HRC 38-45, for Grade 8 fasteners.
technicalDescription: ASTM F436 Type 1 hardened steel washer with 1.5-inch inner diameter. Heat treated to 38-45 HRC. Plain finish with rust-preventive oil. Meets dimensional requirements per ASME B18.22.1.
benefits: Maintains bolt preload in high-vibration applications
seoTitle: Grade 8 Hardened Washer 1.5" ID | Box of 100
seoDescription: ASTM F436 Grade 8 hardened washer, 1.5" ID, HRC 38-45, box of 100. For high-strength bolt assemblies in structural and heavy equipment applications.

faq.question: Why use hardened washers?
faq.answer: Hardened washers distribute clamp load and prevent the bolt head or nut from embedding into the joint surface.

faq.question: Can these be used with Grade 5 bolts?
faq.answer: Yes, but Grade 5 bolts do not require Grade 8 washers for full strength.

faq.question: How should they be oriented?
faq.answer: Washers should be placed under the turning element (nut or bolt head) that is being torqued.

name: ASTM A193 B7 Alloy Steel Stud 3/4-10 x 6"
description: ASTM A193 Grade B7 alloy steel stud bolt for high-temperature and high-pressure flanged connections in process piping. This 3/4-10 x 6-inch continuous thread stud is heat treated and widely used in API and ASME piping applications.
shortDescription: B7 alloy steel stud, 3/4-10 x 6", continuous thread, high-temp service.
technicalDescription: ASTM A193 Grade B7 alloy steel (4140/4142) stud bolt with continuous thread UNC 2A. Quenched and tempered to 125,000 psi minimum tensile. Phosphate coated for corrosion resistance. Supplied with two heavy hex nuts per ASME B18.2.2.
features: ASTM A193 B7 alloy steel, heat treated
Continuous thread design for universal engagement
NACE MR0175 compliant for sour service
benefits: Suitable for high-temperature service up to +450°C
NACE compliant for oil and gas applications
Reliable closure bolting for critical flanged joints
compatibleModels: ASTM A194 Grade 2H Heavy Hex Nuts

documents.name: Stud Bolt Selection Guide for Flanged Joints

seoTitle: ASTM A193 B7 Stud Bolt 3/4-10 x 6"
seoDescription: ASTM A193 Grade B7 alloy steel stud bolt, 3/4-10 x 6", 125 ksi, phosphate coated, NACE compliant. For flanged connections and pressure vessels.

faq.question: What nuts are supplied with this stud?
faq.answer: Two ASTM A194 Grade 2H heavy hex nuts are provided with each stud bolt.

faq.question: What is the maximum service temperature?
faq.answer: ASTM A193 B7 is rated for service from -50°C to +450°C.

faq.question: Is this suitable for hydrogen sulfide service?
faq.answer: Yes, the B7 grade is NACE MR0175 compliant for sour service up to HRC 22 hardness maximum.

name: Grade 8 Threaded Rod 1/2-13 x 36" Zinc Yellow
description: Grade 8 threaded rod made from 4140 alloy steel, heat treated to 150,000 psi tensile. Continuous thread UNC 2A along full 36-inch length. Used for high-strength anchor bolts, tie rods, and heavy equipment fastening.
shortDescription: Grade 8 threaded rod, 1/2-13 x 36", 150 ksi, alloy steel, zinc yellow.
technicalDescription: Continuous thread alloy steel rod per ASTM A354 Grade BD (equivalent to SAE J429 Grade 8). Full length UNC 2A threads. Zinc yellow finish per ASTM B633. Cut-to-length service available.
benefits: High strength for critical anchoring applications
seoDescription: Grade 8 threaded rod, 1/2-13 x 36", 150 ksi, 4140 alloy steel, zinc yellow. For anchor bolts, tie rods, and heavy equipment mounting.

faq.question: Can this be cut to length?
faq.answer: Yes, the rod can be cut with a hacksaw or abrasive saw. Deburr the cut end after cutting.

faq.question: What is the maximum temperature rating?
faq.answer: Grade 8 is rated for service temperatures up to +200°C.

faq.question: What are the torque recommendations?
faq.answer: For Grade 8 1/2-13, recommended torque is 90-110 ft-lbs lubricated. Use a torque wrench for critical applications.

name: L9 Alloy Steel Socket Head Cap Screw 3/8-16 x 2.5"
description: The L9 (Grade 9) socket head cap screw offers the highest strength level available in standard fasteners at 180,000 psi minimum tensile. This 3/8-16 x 2.5-inch bolt is used in extreme loading applications including die clamping and heavy machinery.
shortDescription: L9 socket head cap screw, 3/8-16 x 2.5", 180 ksi, black oxide.
technicalDescription: ASTM A574 alloy steel socket head cap screw with 180,000 psi minimum tensile strength. 4140/4340 alloy steel heat treated to HRC 39-45. Black oxide finish. Internal hex drive per ASME B18.3.
features: Black oxide finish for mild corrosion resistance
benefits: Highest strength standard fastener for extreme loads
Smaller head size allows closer bolt spacing
seoTitle: L9 Socket Head Cap Screw 3/8-16 x 2.5" | 180 ksi
seoDescription: ASTM A574 L9 alloy steel socket head cap screw, 3/8-16 x 2.5", 180,000 psi tensile, black oxide. For extreme loading in die clamping and machinery.

faq.question: What torque should be used?
faq.answer: Recommended tightening torque for L9 3/8-16 lubricated is 45-55 ft-lbs.

faq.question: Can L9 bolts replace Grade 8?
faq.answer: Yes, L9 is stronger (180 ksi vs 150 ksi) and can replace Grade 8 in most applications, but the reverse is not true.

faq.question: Are these suitable for high vibration?
faq.answer: Yes, but use appropriate thread locking compound or mechanical locking features for vibration-prone applications.
